TextENGNew: The downpour outside was growing even more intense.
Thunder was mixing into the heavy rain at ever-shorter intervals.
This was exactly the kind of night you'd call 'a night when something might happen.'
And by 'something,' of course, I didn't mean anything good. Something ominous.
I was restless. There was no way I could calm down.
I just wanted to fall asleep as early as possible.
So I turned off the lights early and crawled under the covers.
Darkness cuts off stray thoughts.
But when negative emotions have taken root deep in your consciousness...
changeReason: Old uses 'subconscious' while new uses 'consciousness,' matching JPN 意識の深層 (deep layer of consciousness); also shifts from lurking imagery to emotions having 'taken root.'

TextENGNew: Stripping away those distractions only makes them sharper, more vivid.
changeReason: Old mistranslates the meaning as positive ('everything will become clear') while new correctly conveys the JPN: stripping away distractions makes the negative emotions sharper and more vivid.

TextENGNew: The negative emotions you shouldn't confront -- anxiety, fear -- are laid bare, and sometimes they attack you outright.
changeReason: Old says 'fear and anxiety I tried to ignore' while new says 'negative emotions you shouldn't confront,' matching JPN 向き合うべきではない (shouldn't confront); changes who is exposed and the framing.

TextENGNew: ...Oyashiro-sama's curse.
No... the Hinamizawa serial murder cases.
changeReason: Old says 'series of deaths' while new says 'serial murder cases,' matching JPN 連続殺人事件; 'deaths' could include natural causes, while 'murders' is more specific.

TextENGNew: ...The Sonozaki family.
...Its de facto leader, Mion Sonozaki.
No... our reliable, good-natured club president, and my friend...
Without meaning to, pieces of the puzzle had floated to the surface.
And yet, I still couldn't make sense of the bigger picture.
What on earth was happening?
Was I already caught up in it?
If so, was there any way out?
Or was it all... just my imagination...?
No, too many disturbing facts had already come to light.
I couldn't possibly dismiss them all as mere delusion.
...And the most important thing to me, of course, was Mion.
Why did I have to be afraid of Mion...?
...Mion was supposed to be my cherished friend.
...But at the same time, the fact that she controlled the Sonozaki family with its vast dark underbelly filled me with dread.
changeReason: Old says 'I can't help but see the darkness behind the Sonozaki family' while new correctly conveys that Mion 'controlled the Sonozaki family,' matching JPN 園崎家を牛耳る存在.

TextENGNew: The connection between the Hinamizawa serial murders and the Sonozaki family...
Mion herself had told me about the dangers of the Sonozaki family, and demanded I keep my mouth shut.
changeReason: Old uses 'warned me not to speak out' while new uses 'demanded I keep my mouth shut,' matching JPN 口外するなと迫った where 迫った implies forceful demanding.

TextENGNew: ...The friends I'd made since coming to Hinamizawa meant everything to me.
I wanted to believe in all of them with absolute trust.
That's right -- I wanted to believe in Mion...!
...My eyes were gradually adjusting to the darkness.
The ceiling was faintly visible.
Imagined images blended with the thinning darkness.
Gradually, they formed shapes that I couldn't tell were real or imagined.
Two sharp, elliptical shapes...!!
I reflexively yanked the blanket over my face.
But hiding under the covers did nothing to settle my nerves.
...What was that just now?!
On the ceiling -- Mion's eyes. No... the eyes of something resembling Mion had flashed in the dark.
That same gaze from earlier today that had rooted me to the spot.
Not Mion's. The hawk-like eyes of someone... something else...
changeReason: Old interprets 鷹の目 as 'predatory eyes' while new translates it literally as 'hawk-like eyes'; 'predatory' implies a broader threat while 'hawk-like' is the specific metaphor used in Japanese.

TextENGNew: What on earth was that...?
A phantom born of fear?
*knock*@k@|@y *knock*@k@|@y *knock*...
I heard a sound. I strained my ears to identify it...
...*knock*!
I threw off the covers and scanned the room in an instant.
No one else was there, but I braced myself anyway.
Both my fists wouldn't stop trembling.
The next moment, there was a knock at the door.
*knock* *knock*.
``Keiichi.@k@vS17/00/112700002. Phone call~. You've got a phone call~.``
``Wh-what...? Mom...?@k@vS17/01/110100304. Okay! I'll be right there!``
When I answered, Mom opened the door.@k All the tension drained from my body at once.
``Honestly, I've been calling you forever.@k@vS17/00/112700004. It's from the bookstore.``
``Huh? The bookstore?``
``Yes, apparently it's urgent.@k@vS17/00/112700006. You're keeping them waiting, so hurry up.``
A bookstore? I didn't recall expecting any calls from one.
changeReason: Old says 'I don't remember giving them my number' while new says 'I didn't recall expecting any calls,' matching JPN 電話を受けるような覚えはない (no memory of expecting a call).

TextENGNew: Something felt off, but I went downstairs and picked up the receiver anyway.
``I apologize for calling at such a late hour.@k@vS17/11/110700053. This is Ooishi from the Okinomiya bookstore.``
I recognized that voice.
``Ooishi-san...?@k@vS17/01/110100307. Ooishi-san... as in Detective Ooishi-san?!``
``Sorry about that.@k@vS17/11/110700055. Your mother answered, so I introduced myself as the bookstore.@k@vS17/11/110700056. People tend to worry when they get a call from the police.``
It seemed Ooishi-san was being considerate in his own way.
changeReason: Old says 'taking extra precautions' (implies security measures) while new says 'being considerate in his own way,' matching JPN 気を遣ってくれた (being considerate/thoughtful).

TextENGNew: ...I switched to the cordless phone and rushed back upstairs to my room.
``Sorry for calling so late.``
``It's fine.@k@vS17/01/110100309. ...So, what happened?``
``Well... this is something we just learned.@k@vS17/11/110700059. It turns out that Shion Sonozaki... has gone missing.``
changeReason: Old says 'about what we discussed earlier' (implying a prior conversation) while new says 'something we just learned,' matching JPN 先ほど解ったこと (something just discovered).

TextENGNew: I nearly let it wash over me, then caught myself and gripped the receiver tightly.
changeReason: Old omits the initial reaction of nearly letting the news pass (JPN さらりと聞き流しそうになって); new captures both the near-miss and the panicked grip.

TextENGNew: ``You're saying Shion is missing...?!``
``That's correct.@k@vS17/11/110700061. And she's been gone for several days now...``
She'd last been sighted several days ago.
That was right around... the night I'd met Mion at the library.
``Maebara-san... do you know anything?``
Could it be that Mion had Shion eliminated...?!
The words almost spilled out before I caught myself and clapped my hand over my mouth.
I remembered the hawk-like eyes Mion had shown me that afternoon.@k ...Mion's warning to keep my mouth shut...
``...N-no, I haven't heard anything.``
``...You really haven't?``
Ooishi-san's low voice rumbled through the receiver like a threat.
``Hmm, the day Shion Sonozaki went missing...@k@vS17/11/110700065. I've received information that she was seen with you, Maebara-san.``
``I-I don't know anything about that...@k@vS17/01/110100316. Unlike Mion, Shion goes to a different school, so I don't see her much.@k@vS17/01/110100317. Maybe they mistook someone else for me?``
``...Nfufufufu, is that so?``
Ooishi-san's sticky, persistent voice clung to my ears uncomfortably.
...Was Ooishi-san suspicious of me?
But I don't know anything! I truly don't!
Then I realized my mistake -- I'd carelessly let Mion's name slip.
I didn't want even the slightest scrap of information about Mion reaching Ooishi-san's ears. ...I didn't want to give Mion or Rena any reason to suspect me.
``Maebara-san... would you please just give me an honest answer?``
``I-I am being honest!@k@vS17/01/110100319. I don't know anything.@k@vS17/01/110100320. I'm not involved in any of this!``
``...Now, now, calm down, Maebara-san.@k@vS17/11/110700069. I'm not asking because I suspect you.``
Ooishi-san's calm voice sounded like he was toying with me in my flustered state.
changeReason: Old says Ooishi 'spoke softly to try to calm me down' while new says his voice 'sounded like he was toying with me,' matching JPN もてあそんでいるように聞こえる.

TextENGNew: If this was some kind of harassment, cut it out. ...Just let me off the phone already!
changeReason: Old uses 'bad joke' while new uses 'harassment,' matching JPN いやがらせ (harassment/bullying), which carries a more hostile connotation.

TextENGNew: ``Nfufufu...@k@vS17/11/110700071. Getting this worked up actually makes you seem more suspicious, you realize?``
``I-I'm sorry!``
``No no, there's nothing to apologize for.@k@vS17/11/110700073. I simply want to confirm whether you truly know nothing.``
``I-I'm telling you!@k@vS17/01/110100324. I don't know anything!``
I didn't know anything...
And yet, Ooishi-san was doubting me...
I couldn't tell Ooishi-san anything about Mion, no matter what...
I desperately tried to organize the thoughts in my head.
``...Are you all right?@k@vS17/11/110700076. Maebara-san?``
``Yeah... anyway, I really don't know anything...``
Then Ooishi-san spoke in a grave tone.
changeReason: Old says 'spoke softly' while new says 'spoke in a grave tone,' matching JPN 神妙な声 (solemn/grave voice), conveying a very different emotional tone.

TextENGNew: ``There's one more thing, actually... her older sister Mion's whereabouts are unknown as well.``
...What?!
``M-Mion too?``
After Shion, Mion had disappeared too...?
``That's right.@k@vS17/11/110700079. And this sort of thing has happened before.``
``Happened before...? Both of them have gone missing before?``
``Yes.@k@vS17/11/110700081. ...It was last year.``
Last year... that recently...?
``When the Houjou family's aunt was murdered, those two suddenly vanished.``
``...You can't be serious...``
...Ooishi-san was talking about the curse on the night of Watanagashi.
``On top of that, the suspect we'd finally gotten a lead on turned up dead around the same time --``
changeReason: Old says 'prime suspect ended up dying' while new says 'suspect we'd finally gotten a lead on turned up dead,' matching JPN せっかく掴んだ容疑者がホトケになってた.

TextENGNew: ...The two of them vanish, and the suspect dies.
It was clear that Ooishi-san suspected them of being behind it.
changeReason: Old says Ooishi 'seemed to suspect' (uncertain) while new says it 'was clear' he suspected them, matching JPN 明白だった (was obvious/clear).

TextENGNew: ``Well, not that it proves anything by itself.``
This year... Tomitake-san had died, and two people had gone missing...
``...If you learn anything, please let me know.``
Ooishi-san's voice suddenly took on a businesslike tone.
``I will...``
changeReason: Old translates はい as 'Understood' (acknowledging information) while new translates it as 'I will' (committing to action), giving a different impression of the speaker's response.

TextENGNew: ``I'll contact you if there are any developments on our end.@k@vS17/11/110700087. ...And Maebara-san, please make sure you contact us if anything comes up, even something minor.@k@vS17/11/110700088. All right, Maebara-san?``
Ooishi-san was implicitly telling me to report immediately if I noticed any movement from the Sonozaki family.
...But if I did that, I'd be the next one killed.
I wanted nothing more than to hang up and escape from Ooishi-san's voice.
``I-I get it!@k@vS17/01/110100331. I understand, okay?``
``...Then I'm counting on you.@k@vS17/11/110700090. ...Well then, good night.``
changeReason: Old says 'And so I have' (unclear referent) while new says 'Then I'm counting on you,' matching JPN ちゃーんと頼みましたよ (I'm properly entrusting this to you).

TextENGNew: *click*
changeReason: Old uses '*clang*' while new uses '*click*' for JPN がちゃり; 'clang' suggests a loud metallic impact while 'click' is the standard phone hang-up sound.

TextENGNew: The moment I hung up, the sound of the downpour outside flooded my ears.
A tremendous crash of thunder boomed amid the heavy rain.
It was deafeningly loud.
And yet, being wrapped in a certain kind of monotonous noise, the world gradually settled into a silence deeper than normal.
changeReason: Old says 'noise was gradually muffled' while new conveys the JPN concept that monotonous sound creates a silence deeper than normal (通常以上の静寂).

TextENGNew: And when that silence became absolute...
My nerves went into overdrive, anticipating the moment it would be shattered.
The sound of rain and thunder.
If I were to hear any other sound...
That would be a sound that should not exist.
changeReason: Old says 'I shouldn't hear anything like that' (focusing on the listener) while new says 'that would be a sound that should not exist,' matching JPN あってはいけない音.

TextENGNew: A sound that would shatter my safety.
The thought of that silence being broken at any moment was enough to fray my nerves to the point of snapping.
I pulled the covers completely over my head.@k But that only made the darkness fuel my imagination all the more.
Mion's hawk-like eyes faintly appeared, hovering just in front of my face.
This time, I was certain it was a hallucination.
Even so, if I didn't fight back the terror with everything I had, I was going to scream.
``--So, what are you going to do?@k@vS17/03/110300278. Keep your mouth shut like this... or?``
Mion's words suddenly resurfaced in my mind.
They felt as real as if she'd whispered them right into my ear.
Every muscle in my body was wound so tight it couldn't possibly get any tighter.
changeReason: Old says 'all my strength escaped me' (weakness) while new says 'wound so tight it couldn't possibly get any tighter' (maximum tension), matching JPN これ以上力が入らないというくらいに緊張した.

TextENGNew: When Mion said 'or?' -- what fate awaited me if I chose wrong?
changeReason: Old only quotes Mion's 'Or...' while new includes the narrator's full thought about what fate awaits him, matching JPN どういう結末が俺を待っているのだろうか.

TextENGNew: Someone acting on Mion's orders might come for me...
changeReason: Old explicitly says 'kill me' while new says 'come for me,' matching the deliberate ambiguity of JPN 俺を…… with trailing ellipsis.

TextENGNew: If they caught me in this room, I wouldn't stand a chance.
Someone might silently open my door and slip inside.
Someone might suddenly smash through the window.
Or perhaps... someone was already inside the room!
I threw off the covers, looked around wildly, and braced myself.
No one was in the room...
Mion and Shion's disappearance -- and apparently it wasn't the first time.
Last time, the Houjou aunt had died at the same time.
changeReason: Old implies the Houjou aunt's death was the main event ('when the aunt died') while new conveys concurrent timing ('died at the same time'), matching JPN 同じタイミングで.

TextENGNew: And on top of that, the suspect had conveniently vanished too.
So what would happen this time...?
Who would die? Who would disappear? How many people would vanish from Hinamizawa this year...?
changeReason: Old uses indefinite 'Someone dies, someone disappears' while new uses interrogative 'Who would die? Who would disappear?' matching JPN 誰が死んで、誰が消える where 誰 means 'who.'

TextENGNew: The downpour and thunder continued unabated.@k There was no sign of morning approaching.
Would I even make it through this night in one piece...?
Would tomorrow morning... truly come...?
Would it really come...?
